signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Risk + Carry + Intervention
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.92 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite US-Iran peace deal headlines weakening the USD elsewhere. WSJ reports of Saudi Arabia and Kuwait lifting US military base restrictions increase regional tension, keeping intervention risk elevated as price holds near the 160.00 psychological barrier. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) confirms a crowded carry trade vulnerable to geopolitical shocks. Carry dominates while VIX remains at 17.1 (normal volatility), but a break above 160.00 triggers BoJ/MoF response risk.

signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Hike Bias
signals.text: NZD/CAD G8 spread at 1.106% identifies a dominant bilateral theme as NZD outperforms (+0.517%) and CAD lags (-0.589%). NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (23.0% long) diverges from the RBNZ hike bias, creating short-squeeze risk if the May 27 meeting confirms hawkishness. CAD remains pressured by vs USD and weak G8 relative strength. NZD/USD at 0.5943; USD/CAD at 1.3659. Watch for RBNZ policy shift to accelerate NZD outperformance.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7212 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) vs retail 88% short identifies a sharp institutional vs retail divergence. Retail is aggressively fading the 4.75% 1M rally while specs remain heavily positioned for further upside, supported by a 60bp AUD carry premium over the Fed. Extreme retail shorting often precedes a final capitulation squeeze higher. Watch for a break of recent highs to trigger retail stop-outs.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Macro Uncertainty
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1733 — Correlation with EuroStoxx at +0.726 (z=+1.82σ) indicates a significant break from the -0.130 norm; EUR is trading as a pro-cyclical risk asset rather than a funding currency. Fed's Williams and Daly emphasize 'uncertainty' and the 2% inflation target, keeping the 175bp USD carry advantage intact. ECB Schnabel's comments on Iran war damage suggest structural headwinds for the Eurozone. Watch for DXY 98.24 support to determine if the EUR/USD 1M rally stalls.
